Barcode rules: All numeric‑looking properties of FieldMergeBarcode (ErrorCorrectionLevel, ScalingFactor, SymbolHeight, SymbolRotation) are strings in recent Aspose.Words versions; provide them as string literals.
Call Document.UpdateFields() after inserting MERGEBARCODE fields to render the barcodes before saving.
Call Update() on the field after setting its properties to render the barcode image.
Keep all Aspose.Words namespaces required for Document, DocumentBuilder, FieldMergeBarcode, and Watermark.
Ensure the MERGEBARCODE field is inserted with builder.InsertField(FieldType.FieldMergeBarcode, true).
Assign string literals to all FieldMergeBarcode properties that expect strings (e.g., colors, numeric options).
Save the document after each modification to persist the barcode fields.
Either add the ZXing.Net NuGet package (dotnet add package ZXing.Net) or replace the runtime barcode generation with a pre‑generated image file.
Ensure the barcode image file (barcode.png) exists at the path specified, or adjust the path accordingly.
Add a reference to the Aspose.BarCode for .NET NuGet package (Aspose.BarCode) – it provides the actual barcode generation implementation.
Remove any usage of System.Drawing.Image; the barcode image is handled as a Stream, eliminating the need for System.Drawing.Common.
Map the string BarcodeType to Aspose.BarCode EncodeTypes inside the custom generator.
Use the DocumentBuilder.InsertField overload that takes only the field code string for DISPLAYBARCODE.
Keep the field code exactly as required: DISPLAYBARCODE "value" type.
Update fields after insertion to render the barcode before extracting images.
Keep the IBarcodeGenerator implementation but ignore the parameters – the example focuses on inserting an image, not on real barcode generation.
All FieldMergeBarcode property values must be supplied as strings, not as Color or numeric types.
Loading rules: Add only the necessary using directives (System, System.IO, Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Saving, Aspose.Words.Loading).
Saving/Export rules: Keep the original workflow: protect, encrypt, load with password, clone, edit, compare, and save.
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Remove all System.Drawing dependencies – they are not available in the default .NET SDK used by the runner.
Replace the on‑the‑fly bitmap generation with a pre‑encoded PNG image (Base64) and insert it via DocumentBuilder.InsertImage(Stream).
Use a minimal placeholder PNG (1×1 pixel) to avoid external files while still demonstrating image insertion.
Add `using System.Drawing;` and `using System.Drawing.Imaging;` for bitmap handling.
Reference the NuGet package `System.Drawing.Common` in the project file to provide the System.Drawing types on non‑Windows platforms.
Reset the memory stream position to 0 before returning it so Aspose.Words can read the image data.
Use the ScalingFactor property of BarcodeParameters to set the image DPI via generator.Parameters.ImageResolution.
Remove all System.Drawing and System.Drawing.Imaging references – they are not available in the default .NET SDK used by the runner.
Watermark rules: Include System.Drawing for Font when customizing watermark text size.
Do not access TextPath.FontSize directly; use Watermark.SetText overload that accepts a Font.
